steps.title: Compare the two predictions
steps.content: Political scientists expected campaign ads to reduce knowledge gaps. Cognitive scientists expected people with more existing political knowledge to learn more easily. The graph shows high-knowledge participants improved after ads, while low-knowledge participants did not meaningfully improve.

steps.title: Connect the widened gap
steps.content: Choice A says the result supports political scientists, but the gap widened. Choice C denies the cognitive phenomenon, though high-knowledge participants benefited more. Choice D says political scientists' prediction occurred, which it did not. The remaining choice states the contrary effect. The answer is B.
